html:
The government has developed a new national cybersecurity strategy. This national cybersecurity strategy outlines the government's approach to issues crucial for Sweden's cybersecurity.

The national cybersecurity strategy is based on national needs and the NIS 2 directive, taking a comprehensive approach to address a wide range of challenges. The strategy details several threats and vulnerabilities that impact Sweden's cybersecurity. It is structured around three key pillars that guide Sweden's cybersecurity efforts:

  • Pillar A: Systematic and effective cybersecurity work.
  • Pillar B: Enhanced knowledge and skills development in cybersecurity.
  • Pillar C: The ability to prevent and manage cybersecurity incidents.

The strategy also includes goals aimed at achieving progress in several areas during the strategy's lifespan, addressing the threats and vulnerabilities outlined within it.

An action plan (Appendix 1) is associated with the strategy, outlining a number of activities that align with the government's approach and the requirements of the NIS 2 directive. This action plan will be updated regularly.
tldr: Sweden has a new plan to protect the country against cyberattacks and IT crimes until the year 2029. The plan aims to make it more difficult for hackers to damage critical systems within society.
lattlast:
The government has created a new plan called the National Cybersecurity Strategy. This plan will guide Sweden's efforts to protect our digital networks and computers between 2025 and 2029. The purpose is to address the threats and vulnerabilities present in our digital world.

The work is based on three key areas:

  • The first area focuses on working more systematically and efficiently to improve security.
  • The second area focuses on becoming better at understanding and learning more about IT security.
  • The third area focuses on becoming more skilled at preventing and managing digital attacks when they occur.

The plan also complies with EU regulations known as the NIS 2 Directive. This is a framework that requires countries and companies to have robust protection against cyberattacks. By following these regulations, Sweden can better manage risks that affect society as a whole.

To implement the plan, there is also an action plan with specific tasks that need to be completed. This action plan will be modified and updated over time to address new types of digital threats that emerge.
